chunk_summary: Guidance on pediatric fecal microbiota transplantation (FMT) referral for recurrent C. difficile infection, including thresholds, pre-referral data, and risk factors.
learning_objectives: Understand the criteria for diagnosing recurrent C. difficile infection in pediatric patients.
Identify appropriate thresholds for FMT referral based on recurrence and severity.
Document pre-referral data, including prior treatments and risk factors, to optimize referral timing.
key_facts: Recurrent C. difficile infection is defined as symptoms returning within 8 weeks after therapy completion.
FMT referral is supported after at least two clinically significant recurrences despite appropriate treatment.
Pre-referral documentation should include prior antibiotic regimens (dose, duration, adherence) and high-risk features like immunocompromise or chronic disease.
Severe or fulminant cases unresponsive to standard therapies warrant earlier FMT consideration.
NASPGHAN/ESPGHAN guidance emphasizes donor screening and post-procedure surveillance in FMT centers.
reference_quote: NASPGHAN/ESPGHAN guidance supports FMT consideration for recurrent infection, particularly after multiple recurrences, or for severe or fulminant disease not responding to standard therapies.
